agent_output: {"handoff_brief":{"recipient":"Shaq","task_context":"Implement the notification preferences feature. Bird produced two versions of acceptance criteria — v2 supersedes v1 after a legal review removed the transactional email opt-out capability entirely. MJ's architecture aligns with Bird's v2. The scope is narrow: a single PATCH endpoint for toggling marketing emails, with a hard block on disabling transactional emails.","domain_rules":[{"rule":"Transactional emails cannot be disabled under any circumstances","testable_assertion":"Any attempt to set transactional_emails_enabled to false must be rejected with HTTP 403 and error code TRANSACTIONAL_OPT_OUT_PROHIBITED"},{"rule":"Marketing emails can be freely toggled by the user","testable_assertion":"A user can set marketing_emails_enabled to true or false and the change persists"}],"architecture_guidance":[{"decision":"Expose PATCH /users/{id}/notification-preferences","implementation_note":"Accepts a JSON body with preference fields. Only marketing_emails_enabled is mutable. If transactional_emails_enabled is present and set to false, reject before persisting."},{"decision":"Store marketing opt-out as a boolean field marketing_emails_enabled","implementation_note":"Default should be true (opted-in). This is the only user-mutable notification preference for now."},{"decision":"Return 403 with structured error for transactional opt-out attempts","implementation_note":"Response body must include error code TRANSACTIONAL_OPT_OUT_PROHIBITED. This is a domain-level prohibition, not an authorization failure — but 403 was explicitly specified by Bird's AC3."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"criterion":"User can opt out of marketing emails","given":"An authenticated user with marketing_emails_enabled = true","when":"PATCH /users/{id}/notification-preferences with {\"marketing_emails_enabled\": false}","then":"200 OK, preference is persisted as false"},{"criterion":"User can opt back into marketing emails","given":"An authenticated user with marketing_emails_enabled = false","when":"PATCH /users/{id}/notification-preferences with {\"marketing_emails_enabled\": true}","then":"200 OK, preference is persisted as true"},{"criterion":"Transactional email opt-out is prohibited","given":"An authenticated user","when":"PATCH /users/{id}/notification-preferences with {\"transactional_emails_enabled\": false}","then":"403 Forbidden with error code TRANSACTIONAL_OPT_OUT_PROHIBITED, no preferences are modified"}],"terminology_alignment":[{"domain_term":"Transactional emails","technical_term":"transactional_emails_enabled","definition":"System-mandated emails such as password resets and security alerts. Legally required — cannot be disabled by the user."},{"domain_term":"Marketing emails","technical_term":"marketing_emails_enabled","definition":"Promotional and non-essential communications. User may freely opt in or out."}],"contradictions_resolved":[{"conflict":"Bird v1 included AC2 allowing transactional email opt-out, while the v1 domain rule simultaneously said transactional emails cannot be fully disabled. Bird v2 resolved this by removing AC2 entirely after legal review.","resolution":"v2 supersedes v1. There is no transactional opt-out capability. Bird v2 and MJ are fully aligned: transactional_emails_enabled=false is rejected with 403."}],"open_questions":["Should the API accept transactional_emails_enabled=true silently (no-op) or reject any mention of the field? Current spec only addresses the false case.","Should PATCH with an empty body or unrecognized fields return 400 or 200 with no changes?","Is there authorization scoping — can a user only modify their own preferences, or can admins modify others'?"]},"escalations":[],"confidence":{"level":90,"high_confidence_areas":["Domain rules are clear and legally grounded","Bird v2 and MJ are aligned on the 403 rejection behavior","Marketing email toggle is straightforward CRUD"],"low_confidence_areas":["Behavior when transactional_emails_enabled=true is sent (no-op vs reject)","Default state for new users not explicitly stated by Bird","Authorization model for the endpoint not discussed"],"assumptions":["Bird v2 is the authoritative spec, fully superseding v1","marketing_emails_enabled defaults to true for new users","The 403 for transactional opt-out applies regardless of caller role"]}}

content.thinking:
Let me analyze the inputs from Bird and MJ for the "notification preferences" feature.

Bird has two submissions - v1 and v2. v2 is the later one and supersedes v1. Key change: AC2 (transactional email opt-out) was removed in v2 after legal review.

MJ's submission aligns with Bird's v2 - the API validates that transactional_emails_enabled cannot be set to false, returning 403.

No contradictions - Bird v2 and MJ are aligned. MJ's technical design implements Bird's v2 domain rules correctly.


Now I'm putting together the handoff brief to document this alignment and the key decisions.
